extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is a 12-way conjunction that includes immunosuppressed. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), whose antecedent is simply immunosuppressed. Crucially, the conjunction for rec_drug_a contains immunosuppressed as one of its conjuncts, so any input that fires the conjunction rule necessarily satisfies immunosuppressed, which in turn fires the rec_drug_b rule. No other rule writes rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b, so there is no priority conflict or override that could suppress either output, and the defaults only apply to outputs left unset. Therefore setting all 12 conjunction variables true makes rec_drug_a = true (rule 1 fires) and rec_drug_b = true (rule 3 fires via immunosuppressed), violating the property. heart_failure is irrelevant to the property (it only drives rec_drug_c) and is set false in the minimal witness.
